findings.name: Authentication bypass via Node.js websocket module
findings.description: An authentication bypass using an alternate path (CWE-288) in FortiOS allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to gain super-admin privileges via crafted requests to the Node.js websocket module. Actively exploited in the wild since November 2024.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to FortiOS 7.0.17 or later. Restrict management interface access to trusted IPs. Disable HTTP/HTTPS admin access on WAN interfaces.

findings.name: SSL VPN out-of-bounds write (RCE)
findings.description: An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in FortiOS SSL VPN (sslvpnd) allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted HTTP requests. Added to CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalogue.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version for your branch. If immediate patching is not possible, disable SSL VPN as a workaround.

findings.name: Format string vulnerability in fgfmd daemon
findings.description: A format string vulnerability (CWE-134) in the FortiOS fgfmd daemon allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted requests.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version. Restrict access to the FGFM port (tcp/541) to trusted FortiManager IPs only.

findings.name: SSL VPN heap buffer overflow (xortigate)
findings.description: A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in FortiOS SSL VPN allows a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code via specially crafted requests. Publicly known as 'xortigate'.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version for your branch. Disable SSL VPN if not required.

findings.name: FortiManager missing authentication (FortiJump)
findings.description: A missing authentication for critical function vulnerability (CWE-306) in FortiManager fgfmsd daemon allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code via specially crafted requests. Known as 'FortiJump'.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade FortiManager to the fixed version. Restrict FGFM access to known FortiGate IP addresses.

findings.name: FortiClient EMS SQL injection
findings.description: An SQL injection vulnerability in FortiClient EMS allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute commands via specially crafted requests to the DAS component.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade FortiClient EMS to the fixed version.

findings.name: Management interface authentication bypass
findings.description: An authentication bypass in the FortiOS management interface allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to gain super-admin privileges.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to FortiOS 7.0.17+. Restrict management access to trusted internal networks only.

findings.name: SSL VPN session hijacking
findings.description: A session fixation vulnerability in FortiOS SSL VPN allows an attacker to hijack user sessions via phishing.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version. Enable MFA for all SSL VPN users.

findings.name: SSL VPN sensitive information disclosure
findings.description: A sensitive information disclosure vulnerability in FortiOS SSL VPN may expose session or configuration data.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version.

findings.name: Access control vulnerability in FortiOS REST API
findings.description: An improper access control vulnerability in FortiOS allows an authenticated attacker to access resources beyond their intended permissions via crafted API requests.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version. Review API user permissions.

findings.name: Captive portal buffer overflow
findings.description: A heap-based buffer overflow in the FortiOS captive portal may allow an attacker with access to the captive portal to execute arbitrary code via specially crafted HTTP requests.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version. Restrict captive portal access.

findings.name: Stack-based buffer overflow in FortiOS IPS
findings.description: A stack-based buffer overflow in FortiOS IPS engine allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to achieve denial of service via crafted packets.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version.

findings.name: Path traversal in FortiOS httpd — file read
findings.description: A path traversal vulnerability in FortiOS httpd allows a remote authenticated attacker with read-only admin privileges to read arbitrary files on the device.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to FortiOS 7.4.5, 7.2.10, or 7.0.16. Restrict admin access to trusted IPs.

findings.name: CSF proxy authentication bypass — super-admin
findings.description: An authentication bypass in FortiOS CSF proxy allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to gain super-admin privileges via crafted CSF proxy requests. Related to CVE-2024-55591, actively exploited.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to FortiOS 7.0.17+. Disable HTTP/HTTPS management on WAN interfaces. Restrict management to trusted IPs.

findings.name: Resource consumption via crafted HTTP/S requests
findings.description: Uncontrolled resource consumption in FortiOS allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to cause denial of service via crafted HTTP/S requests consuming excessive memory.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to the fixed version. Implement rate limiting on management interfaces.

findings.name: Weak authentication in FGFM protocol
findings.description: Weak authentication in the FGFM (FortiGate-FortiManager) protocol allows an attacker with network access to forge communications between FortiGate and FortiManager.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to fixed version. Restrict FGFM port (541) access. Use certificate-based FortiManager auth.

findings.name: SSL VPN improper access control — cross-user
findings.description: Improper access control in FortiOS SSL VPN web portal allows an authenticated attacker to access bookmarks and resources of other SSL VPN users.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to fixed version. Review SSL VPN portal access controls and user group assignments.

findings.name: Authenticated admin privilege escalation
findings.description: Improper privilege management in FortiOS allows an authenticated administrator with limited privileges to escalate to super-admin via crafted configuration changes.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to fixed version. Review admin accounts for least-privilege. Monitor config change logs.

findings.name: HTTP admin access may be enabled
findings.description: Administrative access via HTTP exposes credentials in cleartext.
findings.recommendation: Disable HTTP admin access. Use HTTPS only with admin-https-redirect enabled.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set admin-https-redirect enable
end

findings.name: Admin idle timeout exceeds 5 minutes
findings.description: Admin idle timeout is set to 30 minutes. CIS recommends 5 minutes or less.
findings.recommendation: Set admintimeout to 5 minutes: config system global / set admintimeout 5.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set admintimeout 5
end

findings.name: SIP ALG helper enabled
findings.description: SIP Application Layer Gateway (ALG) helper is enabled. SIP ALG can introduce VoIP issues and bypass firewall policies.
findings.recommendation: Disable SIP helper unless required for NAT traversal: set sip-helper disable.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set strong-crypto enable
end

findings.name: ECMP max paths excessively high
findings.description: ECMP max paths is 32. Excessively high values can cause unpredictable routing.
findings.recommendation: Set ecmp-max-paths to a value appropriate for your network topology (typically 4-8).
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set fds-statistics disable
end

findings.name: Multicast forwarding enabled
findings.description: Multicast forwarding is enabled. Unless required, it increases the attack surface.
findings.recommendation: Disable multicast forwarding if not required: set multicast-forward disable.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set pre-login-banner enable
end

findings.name: Subnet overlap allowed
findings.description: Subnet overlap is allowed, which can cause routing ambiguity and security policy bypass.
findings.recommendation: Disable subnet overlap: set allow-subnet-overlap disable.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set post-login-banner enable
end

findings.name: Central NAT not enabled
findings.description: Central NAT is not enabled. Per-policy NAT can be harder to audit and maintain consistently.
findings.recommendation: Consider enabling central NAT for easier management: set central-nat enable.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set admin-lockout-threshold 3
  set admin-lockout-duration 300
end

findings.name: Unnamed firewall policies allowed
findings.description: Unnamed policies are allowed. Unnamed policies make audit trails and change management difficult.
findings.recommendation: Require named policies: set gui-allow-unnamed-policy disable.

findings.name: No pre-login banner configured
findings.description: No pre-login banner is displayed before admin authentication. Legal banners may be required for compliance.
findings.recommendation: Enable pre-login banner with an appropriate legal warning: set pre-login-banner enable.

findings.name: No post-login banner configured
findings.description: No post-login banner is displayed after admin authentication.
findings.recommendation: Enable post-login banner for compliance: set post-login-banner enable.

findings.name: Strong crypto not enforced globally
findings.description: Strong crypto is not enforced. Weak ciphers (DES, 3DES, RC4) and protocols (SSLv3, TLSv1.0) may be used for admin and VPN sessions.
findings.recommendation: Enable strong-crypto globally: config system global / set strong-crypto enable.

findings.name: Admin account lockout not configured
findings.description: Admin account lockout is not configured. Unlimited failed login attempts enable brute-force attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set admin-lockout-threshold to 3-5 and admin-lockout-duration to 300+ seconds.

findings.name: Admin HTTPS allows weak TLS versions
findings.description: Admin HTTPS interface allows deprecated TLS version (tlsv1-0). These are vulnerable to POODLE, BEAST, and other attacks.
findings.recommendation: Restrict admin HTTPS to TLS 1.2+: set admin-https-ssl-versions tlsv1-2 tlsv1-3.

findings.name: HTTP admin access on interface
findings.line_content: interface=wan1, allowaccess=https ssh http telnet ping snmp fgfm
findings.description: Interface 'wan1' allows HTTP management access.
findings.recommendation: Remove HTTP from allowaccess. Use HTTPS only.

findings.name: Telnet access on interface
findings.line_content: interface=wan1, allowaccess=https ssh http telnet ping snmp fgfm
findings.description: Interface 'wan1' allows Telnet access (unencrypted).
findings.recommendation: Remove telnet from allowaccess. Use SSH only.

findings.name: PING enabled on WAN interface
findings.description: WAN interface 'wan1' responds to ICMP ping, aiding reconnaissance.
findings.recommendation: Remove ping from WAN interface allowaccess unless required for monitoring.

findings.name: Management protocols on WAN interface
findings.description: WAN interface 'wan1' allows management protocols (snmp, fgfm, https, ssh). This exposes the management plane to the internet.
findings.recommendation: Remove all management protocols from WAN interfaces. Use a dedicated management interface or VPN for admin access.

findings.name: HTTP admin access on interface
findings.description: Interface 'dmz' allows HTTP management access.
findings.recommendation: Remove HTTP from allowaccess. Use HTTPS only.

findings.name: Any-to-any allow policy
findings.description: Policy 'Allow-All-Internet' allows traffic from any source to any destination, bypassing segmentation.
findings.recommendation: Replace with specific source and destination address objects.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config firewall policy
  edit <policy-id>
    set srcaddr <specific-address>
    set dstaddr <specific-address>
    set service <specific-service>
  next
end

findings.name: Allow policy with ALL services
findings.description: Policy 'Allow-All-Internet' allows all services/ports.
findings.recommendation: Restrict services to only those required.

findings.name: No traffic logging on allow policy
findings.description: Policy 'Allow-All-Internet' does not log traffic, hindering forensic investigation.
findings.recommendation: Enable logtraffic=all or logtraffic=utm on all allow policies.

findings.name: Allow policy without security profiles
findings.description: Policy 'Allow-All-Internet' allows traffic without any UTM security profiles (AV, IPS, WebFilter, AppControl).
findings.recommendation: Apply security profiles (antivirus, IPS, web filter, application control) to all allow policies.

findings.name: Allow policy without SSL inspection
findings.description: Policy 'Allow-All-Internet' has no SSL inspection profile. Encrypted threats may bypass security profiles.
findings.recommendation: Apply an ssl-ssh-profile to enable encrypted traffic inspection.

findings.name: Broad allow policy with permanent schedule
findings.description: Policy 'Allow-All-Internet' has a broad scope (all src/dst/svc) with an 'always' schedule, suggesting a temporary rule made permanent.
findings.recommendation: Review if this policy should have a time-limited schedule. Apply least-privilege addressing.

findings.name: Allow policy with 'all' source
findings.description: Policy 'DMZ-Access' allows traffic from any source address.
findings.recommendation: Restrict the source to specific address objects or groups.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config firewall policy
  edit <policy-id>
    set logtraffic all
  next
end

findings.name: No traffic logging on allow policy
findings.description: Policy 'DMZ-Access' does not log traffic, hindering forensic investigation.
findings.recommendation: Enable logtraffic=all or logtraffic=utm on all allow policies.

findings.name: Allow policy without security profiles
findings.description: Policy 'DMZ-Access' allows traffic without any UTM security profiles (AV, IPS, WebFilter, AppControl).
findings.recommendation: Apply security profiles (antivirus, IPS, web filter, application control) to all allow policies.

findings.name: Allow policy without SSL inspection
findings.description: Policy 'DMZ-Access' has no SSL inspection profile. Encrypted threats may bypass security profiles.
findings.recommendation: Apply an ssl-ssh-profile to enable encrypted traffic inspection.

findings.name: Broad allow policy with permanent schedule
findings.description: Policy 'DMZ-Access' has a broad scope (all src/dst/svc) with an 'always' schedule, suggesting a temporary rule made permanent.
findings.recommendation: Review if this policy should have a time-limited schedule. Apply least-privilege addressing.

findings.name: Unnamed firewall policy
findings.description: Firewall policy ID 3 has no name. Unnamed policies are difficult to audit and troubleshoot.
findings.recommendation: Assign descriptive names to all firewall policies.

findings.name: Allow policy with 'all' destination
findings.description: Policy '' allows traffic to any destination address.
findings.recommendation: Restrict the destination to specific address objects or groups.

findings.name: Allow policy with ALL services
findings.description: Policy '' allows all services/ports.
findings.recommendation: Restrict services to only those required.

findings.name: No traffic logging on allow policy
findings.description: Policy '' does not log traffic, hindering forensic investigation.
findings.recommendation: Enable logtraffic=all or logtraffic=utm on all allow policies.

findings.name: Allow policy without security profiles
findings.description: Policy '' allows traffic without any UTM security profiles (AV, IPS, WebFilter, AppControl).
findings.recommendation: Apply security profiles (antivirus, IPS, web filter, application control) to all allow policies.

findings.name: Allow policy without SSL inspection
findings.description: Policy '' has no SSL inspection profile. Encrypted threats may bypass security profiles.
findings.recommendation: Apply an ssl-ssh-profile to enable encrypted traffic inspection.

findings.name: Broad allow policy with permanent schedule
findings.description: Policy '' has a broad scope (all src/dst/svc) with an 'always' schedule, suggesting a temporary rule made permanent.
findings.recommendation: Review if this policy should have a time-limited schedule. Apply least-privilege addressing.

findings.name: Disabled firewall policy
findings.description: Firewall policy 'Old-Test-Policy' (ID 4) is disabled. Disabled policies add clutter and may be accidentally re-enabled.
findings.recommendation: Remove disabled policies or document the reason for keeping them disabled.

findings.name: Deny policy without logging
findings.description: Deny policy 'Deny-Block' does not log blocked traffic. This hinders attack detection.
findings.recommendation: Enable logtraffic on deny policies to record blocked connection attempts.

findings.name: Disabled firewall policy
findings.description: Firewall policy 'Old-Disabled-1' (ID 6) is disabled. Disabled policies add clutter and may be accidentally re-enabled.
findings.recommendation: Remove disabled policies or document the reason for keeping them disabled.

findings.name: Disabled firewall policy
findings.description: Firewall policy 'Old-Disabled-2' (ID 7) is disabled. Disabled policies add clutter and may be accidentally re-enabled.
findings.recommendation: Remove disabled policies or document the reason for keeping them disabled.

findings.name: Disabled firewall policy
findings.description: Firewall policy 'Old-Disabled-3' (ID 8) is disabled. Disabled policies add clutter and may be accidentally re-enabled.
findings.recommendation: Remove disabled policies or document the reason for keeping them disabled.

findings.name: Disabled firewall policy
findings.description: Firewall policy 'Old-Disabled-4' (ID 9) is disabled. Disabled policies add clutter and may be accidentally re-enabled.
findings.recommendation: Remove disabled policies or document the reason for keeping them disabled.

findings.name: Disabled firewall policy
findings.description: Firewall policy 'Old-Disabled-5' (ID 10) is disabled. Disabled policies add clutter and may be accidentally re-enabled.
findings.recommendation: Remove disabled policies or document the reason for keeping them disabled.

findings.name: High ratio of disabled policies
findings.description: 6 of 10 policies (60%) are disabled. Excessive disabled policies indicate poor policy lifecycle management.
findings.recommendation: Review and remove disabled policies that are no longer needed.

findings.name: VIP without port forwarding restriction
findings.description: VIP 'WebServer-VIP' maps all ports from 203.0.113.10 to the internal server. This exposes all services on the target.
findings.recommendation: Enable port forwarding and restrict to only required ports: set portforward enable.

findings.name: No egress filtering policies detected
findings.description: No egress filtering (outbound deny) policies detected. Without egress filtering, compromised hosts can freely exfiltrate data.
findings.recommendation: Implement egress filtering policies to restrict outbound traffic to only necessary services.

findings.name: SSL VPN allows weak TLS version
findings.description: SSL VPN minimum TLS version is set to tlsv1-0, which is deprecated and vulnerable.
findings.recommendation: Set ssl-min-proto-ver to tls1-2 or higher.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config vpn ssl settings
  set sslv3 disable
  set tlsv1-0 disable
  set tlsv1-1 disable
  set tlsv1-2 enable
  set tlsv1-3 enable
end

findings.name: SSL VPN using default port
findings.description: SSL VPN is running on default port 443, making it easily discoverable.
findings.recommendation: Change the SSL VPN port to a non-standard port.

findings.name: SSL VPN idle timeout too long
findings.description: SSL VPN idle timeout is 3600 seconds (60 min). Sessions left open increase hijacking risk.
findings.recommendation: Set idle-timeout to 300 seconds (5 minutes) or less.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config vpn ssl settings
  set reqclientcert enable
end

findings.name: SSL VPN login attempt limit too high
findings.description: SSL VPN allows 10 failed login attempts, enabling brute-force attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set login-attempt-limit to 3 or fewer.

findings.name: SSL VPN split tunnelling enabled
findings.description: Portal 'full-access' allows split tunnelling, sending only specific traffic through VPN. Users may access malicious sites via local internet.
findings.recommendation: Disable split tunnelling to route all traffic through the VPN: set split-tunneling disable.

findings.name: SSL VPN split tunnelling enabled
findings.description: Portal 'guest-portal' allows split tunnelling, sending only specific traffic through VPN. Users may access malicious sites via local internet.
findings.recommendation: Disable split tunnelling to route all traffic through the VPN: set split-tunneling disable.

findings.name: VPN user group without MFA enforcement
findings.description: VPN user group 'VPN-Users' with 3 member(s) does not have clear MFA enforcement. VPN access without MFA is a high-risk vector.
findings.recommendation: Enforce two-factor authentication for all VPN user groups using FortiToken or RADIUS MFA.

findings.name: VPN user group without MFA enforcement
findings.description: VPN user group 'SSL-Remote-Workers' with 2 member(s) does not have clear MFA enforcement. VPN access without MFA is a high-risk vector.
findings.recommendation: Enforce two-factor authentication for all VPN user groups using FortiToken or RADIUS MFA.

findings.name: No SSL VPN tunnel IP pool configured
findings.description: No IP pool is configured for SSL VPN tunnel mode. Tunnel mode clients may not receive proper IP assignments.
findings.recommendation: Configure dedicated tunnel IP pools for SSL VPN clients.

findings.name: No DNS server configured for VPN clients
findings.description: No DNS servers are pushed to SSL VPN clients. Clients may use their local DNS, causing DNS leaks.
findings.recommendation: Configure internal DNS servers for VPN clients: set dns-server1 <IP>.

findings.name: DTLS tunnel disabled
findings.description: DTLS (Datagram TLS) tunnel is disabled. DTLS provides better VPN performance for latency-sensitive applications.
findings.recommendation: Enable dtls-tunnel for improved VPN throughput on UDP-based traffic.

findings.name: No SSL VPN banned cipher suites configured
findings.description: No cipher suites are banned for SSL VPN. Weak ciphers (RC4, DES, NULL) may be negotiated.
findings.recommendation: Ban weak ciphers: set banned-cipher RSA DES-CBC-SHA RC4 NULL.

findings.name: SSL VPN portal without host check
findings.description: SSL VPN portal 'full-access' does not enforce host checking. Unmanaged/compromised endpoints can connect.
findings.recommendation: Enable host-check to verify endpoint compliance (AV, OS version, etc.): set host-check enable.

findings.name: SSL VPN portal allows FTP access
findings.description: SSL VPN portal 'full-access' allows FTP access, enabling unmonitored file transfers.
findings.recommendation: Restrict user access to only necessary protocols. Remove FTP if not required.

findings.name: SSL VPN portal without concurrent login limit
findings.description: SSL VPN portal 'full-access' does not limit concurrent logins per user. Credential sharing goes undetected.
findings.recommendation: Enable concurrent login limits: set limit-user-logins enable.

findings.name: SSL VPN portal without concurrent login limit
findings.description: SSL VPN portal 'guest-portal' does not limit concurrent logins per user. Credential sharing goes undetected.
findings.recommendation: Enable concurrent login limits: set limit-user-logins enable.

findings.name: SSL VPN HTTP compression enabled (CRIME/BREACH risk)
findings.description: HTTP compression over SSL VPN is enabled. Compression combined with TLS is vulnerable to CRIME/BREACH attacks.
findings.recommendation: Disable HTTP compression for SSL VPN: set http-compression disable.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 aggressive mode
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses aggressive mode, which sends identity in cleartext and is susceptible to offline brute-force attacks.
findings.recommendation: Use main mode (IKEv1) or IKEv2 instead of aggressive mode.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config vpn ipsec phase1-interface
  edit <vpn-name>
    set proposal aes256-sha256 aes256gcm-prfsha384
  next
end

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 weak encryption
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses weak encryption algorithm 'des-md5'.
findings.recommendation: Use AES-256 or AES-128 encryption. Remove DES, 3DES, RC4.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config vpn ipsec phase1-interface
  edit <vpn-name>
    set dhgrp 14 20 21
  next
end

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 weak hash algorithm
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses weak hash algorithm in 'des-md5'.
findings.recommendation: Use SHA-256 or SHA-512 hashing. Remove MD5.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 weak encryption
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses weak encryption algorithm '3des-md5'.
findings.recommendation: Use AES-256 or AES-128 encryption. Remove DES, 3DES, RC4.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config vpn ipsec phase1-interface
  edit <vpn-name>
    set dhgrp 14 20 21
  next
end

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 weak hash algorithm
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses weak hash algorithm in '3des-md5'.
findings.recommendation: Use SHA-256 or SHA-512 hashing. Remove MD5.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 weak DH group
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses weak Diffie-Hellman group 1.
findings.recommendation: Use DH group 14 (2048-bit) or higher. Remove groups 1, 2, and 5.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 weak DH group
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses weak Diffie-Hellman group 2.
findings.recommendation: Use DH group 14 (2048-bit) or higher. Remove groups 1, 2, and 5.

findings.name: IPsec Dead Peer Detection disabled
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' has DPD disabled, which may leave stale tunnels consuming resources.
findings.recommendation: Enable DPD: set dpd on-demand or on-idle.

findings.name: IPsec using IKEv1 (legacy protocol)
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses IKEv1. IKEv2 provides better security, performance, and NAT traversal.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to IKEv2: set ike-version 2.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 keylife too long
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' Phase 1 keylife is 172800 seconds (48 hours). Long key lifetimes increase the window for key compromise.
findings.recommendation: Set Phase 1 keylife to 28800 seconds (8 hours) or less.

findings.name: IPsec using pre-shared key authentication
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Site-B-Tunnel' uses pre-shared key (PSK) authentication instead of certificates.
findings.recommendation: Use certificate-based authentication for stronger identity verification: set authmethod signature.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 weak encryption
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Cloud-VPN' uses weak encryption algorithm '3des-sha256'.
findings.recommendation: Use AES-256 or AES-128 encryption. Remove DES, 3DES, RC4.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config vpn ipsec phase1-interface
  edit <vpn-name>
    set dhgrp 14 20 21
  next
end

findings.name: IPsec Phase 1 weak DH group
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Cloud-VPN' uses weak Diffie-Hellman group 5.
findings.recommendation: Use DH group 14 (2048-bit) or higher. Remove groups 1, 2, and 5.

findings.name: IPsec using IKEv1 (legacy protocol)
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Cloud-VPN' uses IKEv1. IKEv2 provides better security, performance, and NAT traversal.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to IKEv2: set ike-version 2.

findings.name: IPsec using pre-shared key authentication
findings.description: IPsec tunnel 'Cloud-VPN' uses pre-shared key (PSK) authentication instead of certificates.
findings.recommendation: Use certificate-based authentication for stronger identity verification: set authmethod signature.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 2 PFS disabled
findings.description: IPsec Phase 2 'Site-B-P2' has Perfect Forward Secrecy disabled. Compromised keys can decrypt past sessions.
findings.recommendation: Enable PFS: set pfs enable with DH group 14 or higher.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 2 weak encryption
findings.description: IPsec Phase 2 'Site-B-P2' uses weak encryption 'des-md5'.
findings.recommendation: Use AES-256 or AES-128 for Phase 2 encryption. Remove DES, 3DES.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 2 keylife too long
findings.description: IPsec Phase 2 'Site-B-P2' keylife is 86400 seconds. Long key lifetimes increase compromise risk.
findings.recommendation: Set Phase 2 keylifeseconds to 3600-28800 seconds.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 2 replay protection disabled
findings.description: IPsec Phase 2 'Site-B-P2' has anti-replay protection disabled, allowing packet replay attacks.
findings.recommendation: Enable anti-replay: set replay enable.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 2 PFS disabled
findings.description: IPsec Phase 2 'Cloud-VPN-P2' has Perfect Forward Secrecy disabled. Compromised keys can decrypt past sessions.
findings.recommendation: Enable PFS: set pfs enable with DH group 14 or higher.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 2 weak encryption
findings.description: IPsec Phase 2 'Cloud-VPN-P2' uses weak encryption '3des-sha256'.
findings.recommendation: Use AES-256 or AES-128 for Phase 2 encryption. Remove DES, 3DES.

findings.name: IPsec Phase 2 keylife too long
findings.description: IPsec Phase 2 'Cloud-VPN-P2' keylife is 50000 seconds. Long key lifetimes increase compromise risk.
findings.recommendation: Set Phase 2 keylifeseconds to 3600-28800 seconds.

findings.name: AV profile HTTP not in block mode
findings.description: AV profile 'default' HTTP scanning is set to 'monitor' instead of blocking infected files.
findings.recommendation: Set HTTP AV scanning to block mode to prevent malware delivery.

findings.name: AV outbreak prevention disabled
findings.description: AV profile 'default' does not have outbreak prevention enabled for zero-day malware detection.
findings.recommendation: Enable outbreak prevention to detect unknown threats via FortiGuard sandbox.

findings.name: AV cloud sandbox analysis disabled
findings.description: AV profile 'default' does not submit suspicious files to FortiGuard cloud sandbox.
findings.recommendation: Enable FortiGuard analytics for sandbox-based zero-day detection.

findings.name: IPS sensor in monitor-only mode
findings.description: IPS sensor 'default-ips' has all entries set to pass/monitor. No traffic is being blocked.
findings.recommendation: Set IPS entries to 'block' for critical and high severity signatures.

findings.name: IPS sensor has IP exemptions
findings.description: IPS sensor 'default-ips' has 1 entries with IP exemptions. Exempted IPs bypass IPS inspection.
findings.recommendation: Review and minimize IPS exemptions. Remove any that are no longer necessary.

findings.name: Web filter safe search not enforced
findings.description: Web filter 'basic-filter' does not enforce safe search on search engines.
findings.recommendation: Enable safe search enforcement to filter explicit content from search results.

findings.name: Web filter has no category filters
findings.description: Web filter 'basic-filter' has no FortiGuard category filters configured.
findings.recommendation: Configure category-based filtering to block malware, phishing, and high-risk sites.

findings.name: High-risk app categories allowed
findings.description: Application control 'app-default' allows high-risk categories (P2P, proxy, botnet).
findings.recommendation: Block high-risk application categories including P2P, proxy bypass, and botnet tools.

findings.name: DLP sensor in log-only mode
findings.description: DLP sensor 'basic-dlp' only logs data leaks without blocking. Sensitive data can still leave the network.
findings.recommendation: Set DLP filter actions to 'block' for critical data patterns (PII, financial, healthcare).

findings.name: DNS filter botnet protection disabled
findings.description: DNS filter 'dns-default' does not block botnet C2 domains.
findings.recommendation: Enable botnet domain blocking in DNS filter profiles.

findings.name: SSL inspection profile not in deep inspection mode
findings.description: SSL inspection profile 'custom-ssl-profile' does not perform deep content inspection. Encrypted malware may pass through undetected.
findings.recommendation: Enable deep SSL inspection for high-risk traffic. Use certificate inspection only where deep inspection causes compatibility issues.

findings.name: SSL profile allows invalid server certificates
findings.description: SSL profile 'custom-ssl-profile' allows connections to servers with invalid certificates, enabling MitM attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set invalid-server-cert action to 'block' to prevent MitM attacks.

findings.name: No email filter profiles configured
findings.description: No email filter profiles are configured. Email-borne threats (phishing, malware) are not inspected.
findings.recommendation: Create email filter profiles if SMTP traffic passes through the FortiGate.

findings.name: File filter profile with no rules
findings.description: File filter profile 'file-default' has no file type rules. No file types are being blocked.
findings.recommendation: Add rules to block dangerous file types (exe, bat, scr, dll, vbs, ps1).

findings.name: ICAP profile without preview mode
findings.description: ICAP profile 'icap-server' does not use preview mode. All traffic is sent to the ICAP server, increasing latency.
findings.recommendation: Enable preview mode for ICAP to send only the initial bytes for faster content decisions.

findings.name: FortiAnalyzer not configured
findings.description: FortiAnalyzer is not enabled for centralized log management and analysis.
findings.recommendation: Configure FortiAnalyzer for centralized logging: config log fortianalyzer setting / set status enable.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config log fortianalyzer setting
  set status enable
  set server <FAZ-IP>
end

findings.name: Syslog forwarding not configured
findings.description: Syslog forwarding is not enabled. Logs stored only locally may be lost if the device is compromised.
findings.recommendation: Configure syslog forwarding to a SIEM or log management platform.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config log syslogd setting
  set status enable
  set server <syslog-IP>
end

findings.name: Log disk full action set to overwrite
findings.description: When log disk is full, older logs are overwritten, potentially destroying forensic evidence.
findings.recommendation: Set diskfull action to 'nolog' or configure log forwarding to prevent data loss.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config log setting
  set fwpolicy-implicit-log enable
end

findings.name: Implicit deny logging disabled
findings.description: Traffic denied by the implicit deny rule is not logged, hiding potential attack attempts.
findings.recommendation: Enable implicit deny logging: set fwpolicy-implicit-log enable.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config log fortianalyzer setting
  set enc-algorithm high
end

findings.name: Local-in traffic logging disabled
findings.description: Traffic destined to the FortiGate itself (local-in) is not logged. Admin and management access attempts go unrecorded.
findings.recommendation: Enable local-in-allow logging: set local-in-allow enable.

findings.name: Local-in deny logging disabled
findings.description: Denied local-in unicast traffic is not logged. Reconnaissance and attack attempts on the firewall itself go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Enable local-in-deny-unicast logging: set local-in-deny-unicast enable.

findings.name: System event logging disabled
findings.description: System event logging is disabled. Configuration changes, admin logins, and system alerts are not recorded.
findings.recommendation: Enable system event logging: config log eventfilter / set event enable.

findings.name: VPN event logging disabled
findings.description: VPN event logging is disabled. VPN login failures, tunnel status changes, and session events are not recorded.
findings.recommendation: Enable VPN event logging: set vpn enable.

findings.name: User authentication event logging disabled
findings.description: User authentication event logging is disabled. Login attempts and authentication failures are not recorded.
findings.recommendation: Enable user event logging: set user enable.

findings.name: Alert email not configured
findings.description: No alert email recipients configured. Critical security events will not trigger email notifications.
findings.recommendation: Configure alert email recipients: config alertemail setting / set mailto1 <email>.

findings.name: No automation triggers configured
findings.description: No automation triggers are configured. Automated responses to security events (IPS alerts, admin logins, HA failover) are not set up.
findings.recommendation: Configure automation triggers and actions for critical security events.

findings.name: HA heartbeat not authenticated
findings.description: HA heartbeat communication is not authenticated, allowing potential cluster hijacking.
findings.recommendation: Enable HA authentication: set authentication enable / set password <strong-password>.

findings.name: HA session pickup disabled
findings.description: Session pickup is disabled. Active sessions will be dropped during HA failover.
findings.recommendation: Enable session-pickup for seamless failover: set session-pickup enable.

findings.name: HA cluster communication not encrypted
findings.description: HA cluster communication is not encrypted. Configuration and session data exchanged between peers may be intercepted.
findings.recommendation: Enable HA encryption: config system ha / set encryption enable.

findings.name: No dedicated HA heartbeat interface
findings.description: No dedicated heartbeat interface is configured. HA heartbeat shares bandwidth with data traffic.
findings.recommendation: Configure dedicated HA heartbeat interfaces: set hbdev <interface> <priority>.

findings.name: No HA monitored interfaces configured
findings.description: No interfaces are monitored for HA failover. Link failures on critical interfaces will not trigger failover.
findings.recommendation: Configure monitored interfaces: set monitor <wan-interface> <lan-interface>.

findings.name: HA override enabled (auto-failback)
findings.description: HA override is enabled. The primary unit will automatically reclaim the primary role after recovery, causing a second failover.
findings.recommendation: Consider disabling override to prevent unnecessary failover disruptions: set override disable.

findings.name: HA cluster firmware version mismatch
findings.description: HA cluster members are running different firmware versions: v7.0.8, v7.0.10.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade all HA cluster members to the same firmware version.

findings.name: Default Fortinet factory certificate in use
findings.description: The default Fortinet factory certificate 'Fortinet_Factory_Self-Sign' is in use. This is a known, shared certificate.
findings.recommendation: Replace the factory certificate with a CA-signed certificate specific to your organisation.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config vpn certificate local
  Generate or import a CA-signed certificate to replace the default self-signed certificate.
end

findings.name: Certificate with weak key size
findings.description: Certificate 'Fortinet_Factory_Self-Sign' uses a 1024-bit key. Keys shorter than 2048 bits are considered weak.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a certificate using at least 2048-bit RSA or 256-bit ECDSA key.

findings.name: Certificate using SHA-1 signature algorithm
findings.description: Certificate 'Fortinet_Factory_Self-Sign' uses SHA-1 signature algorithm, which is deprecated and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a certificate using SHA-256 or SHA-384 signature algorithm.

findings.name: Expired certificate
findings.description: Certificate 'Fortinet_Factory_Self-Sign' expired on 2020-01-01 00:00:00.
findings.recommendation: Replace the expired certificate immediately.

findings.name: Certificate expiring within 30 days
findings.description: Certificate 'corp-wildcard' will expire on 2026-04-15 00:00:00.
findings.recommendation: Renew the certificate before expiry.

findings.name: Wildcard certificate in use
findings.description: Certificate 'corp-wildcard' is a wildcard certificate (*.corp.example.com). Wildcard certs increase blast radius if compromised.
findings.recommendation: Use specific certificates per service where possible. Monitor wildcard cert usage closely.

findings.name: Self-signed certificate detected
findings.description: Certificate 'internal-ca' is self-signed. Self-signed certificates cannot be verified by clients and are vulnerable to MitM attacks.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a CA-signed certificate from a trusted Certificate Authority.

findings.name: No certificate revocation checking configured
findings.description: No CRL or OCSP servers are configured. Revoked certificates will still be accepted as valid.
findings.recommendation: Configure CRL distribution points or OCSP servers for certificate revocation checking.

findings.name: DoS SYN flood threshold too high
findings.description: DoS policy 1 SYN flood threshold is 50000 (>10,000). High thresholds may not protect against moderate floods.
findings.recommendation: Lower the SYN flood threshold to 2,000-5,000 per second based on normal traffic baselines.

findings.name: DoS UDP flood threshold too high
findings.description: DoS policy 1 UDP flood threshold is 50000. High thresholds reduce protection effectiveness.
findings.recommendation: Lower the UDP flood threshold based on traffic baselines.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system ntp
  set ntpsync enable
  set type custom
  config ntpserver
    edit 1
      set server <ntp-server>
    next
  end
end

findings.name: DoS ICMP flood threshold too high
findings.description: DoS policy 1 ICMP flood threshold is 5000.
findings.recommendation: Lower ICMP flood threshold to 250-500 per second.

findings.name: DHCP relay on WAN interface
findings.description: WAN interface 'wan1' has DHCP relay enabled, potentially exposing internal DHCP to the internet.
findings.recommendation: Disable DHCP relay on WAN interfaces unless explicitly required.

findings.name: DNS server override disabled on WAN
findings.description: WAN interface 'wan1' does not override DNS from ISP. ISP DNS may not filter malicious domains.
findings.recommendation: Use FortiGuard DNS or a reputable DNS service and enable dns-server-override.

findings.name: Source IP check disabled on external interface
findings.description: Interface 'wan1' has source IP check (RPF/anti-spoofing) disabled, allowing spoofed packets.
findings.recommendation: Enable src-check on all WAN and DMZ interfaces: set src-check enable.

findings.name: Source IP check disabled on external interface
findings.description: Interface 'dmz' has source IP check (RPF/anti-spoofing) disabled, allowing spoofed packets.
findings.recommendation: Enable src-check on all WAN and DMZ interfaces: set src-check enable.

findings.name: TCP sessions without SYN allowed
findings.description: FortiGate accepts TCP sessions without SYN, which can bypass firewall stateful inspection.
findings.recommendation: Disable tcp-session-without-syn: config system global / set tcp-session-without-syn disable.

findings.name: IPv6 enabled but no IPv6 firewall policies
findings.description: IPv6 is enabled on the FortiGate but no IPv6 firewall policies exist, potentially allowing unfiltered IPv6 traffic.
findings.recommendation: Create IPv6 firewall policies or disable IPv6 if not required.

findings.name: LLDP reception enabled globally
findings.description: LLDP reception is enabled globally. LLDP can leak network topology information to adjacent devices.
findings.recommendation: Disable LLDP on untrusted interfaces. Use per-interface LLDP settings.

findings.name: BGP neighbour without authentication
findings.description: BGP neighbour 10.0.0.1 has no MD5 authentication, allowing BGP hijacking.
findings.recommendation: Configure BGP MD5 authentication for all neighbours: set password <key>.

findings.name: BGP neighbour without authentication
findings.description: BGP neighbour 10.0.0.2 has no MD5 authentication, allowing BGP hijacking.
findings.recommendation: Configure BGP MD5 authentication for all neighbours: set password <key>.

findings.name: OSPF interface without authentication
findings.description: OSPF interface 'lan-ospf' has no authentication, allowing rogue router injection.
findings.recommendation: Enable OSPF authentication: set authentication md5 or text.

findings.name: OSPF interface without authentication
findings.description: OSPF interface 'dmz-ospf' has no authentication, allowing rogue router injection.
findings.recommendation: Enable OSPF authentication: set authentication md5 or text.

findings.name: SNMP default community string
findings.description: SNMP community string 'public' is a well-known default. Attackers can read device configuration.
findings.recommendation: Change SNMP community strings to complex values or migrate to SNMPv3 with authentication.

findings.name: SNMPv1 enabled
findings.description: SNMPv1 is enabled for community 'public'. SNMPv1 transmits data in cleartext without authentication.
findings.recommendation: Disable SNMPv1 and migrate to SNMPv3 with authPriv.

findings.name: SNMP default community string
findings.description: SNMP community string 'private' is a well-known default. Attackers can read device configuration.
findings.recommendation: Change SNMP community strings to complex values or migrate to SNMPv3 with authentication.

findings.name: SNMPv3 user without authentication
findings.description: SNMPv3 user 'monitor-user' has no authentication or encryption.
findings.recommendation: Set SNMPv3 user security level to auth-priv with SHA and AES.

findings.name: NTP authentication not enabled
findings.description: NTP authentication is not enabled. Attackers can inject false time via NTP spoofing, breaking log correlation and certificates.
findings.recommendation: Enable NTP authentication: config system ntp / set authentication enable.

findings.name: ZTNA access proxy with no API gateway rules
findings.description: ZTNA access proxy 'ztna-proxy-1' has no API gateway rules configured.
findings.recommendation: Configure API gateway rules to define application access policies.

findings.name: ZTNA access proxy without client certificate
findings.description: ZTNA access proxy 'ztna-proxy-1' does not require client certificates for device identity verification.
findings.recommendation: Enable client certificate requirement: set client-cert enable.

findings.name: SD-WAN enabled without health checks
findings.description: SD-WAN is enabled but no health checks are configured. SD-WAN cannot detect link quality issues without health checks.
findings.recommendation: Configure SD-WAN health checks (ping, HTTP, DNS) for all WAN links.

findings.name: SD-WAN without service/SLA rules
findings.description: SD-WAN has no service/SLA rules configured. Traffic will use default routing without quality-based path selection.
findings.recommendation: Configure SD-WAN service rules with SLA targets for critical applications.

findings.name: FortiGuard antivirus service expired
findings.description: FortiGuard service 'antivirus' is expired. Security signatures may be outdated.
findings.recommendation: Renew the FortiGuard antivirus license to ensure up-to-date threat protection.
findings.remediation_cmd: Upgrade FortiOS firmware via System > Firmware. Download from https://support.fortinet.com

findings.name: FortiGuard ips licence expiring soon
findings.description: FortiGuard 'ips' licence expires on 2026-04-10. Renewal is needed to maintain protection.
findings.recommendation: Renew the ips licence before it expires.

findings.name: FortiGuard ips signatures outdated
findings.description: FortiGuard ips signatures are 120 days old. AV/IPS signatures should update daily.
findings.recommendation: Check FortiGuard connectivity and automatic update schedule. Signatures should update at least daily.

findings.name: FortiGuard webfilter service disabled
findings.description: FortiGuard service 'webfilter' is disabled. Security signatures may be outdated.
findings.recommendation: Renew the FortiGuard webfilter license to ensure up-to-date threat protection.
findings.remediation_cmd: Upgrade FortiOS firmware via System > Firmware. Download from https://support.fortinet.com

findings.name: FortiGuard app-ctrl not updated in 44 days
findings.description: FortiGuard app-ctrl has not been updated in 44 days.
findings.recommendation: Verify FortiGuard update schedule and connectivity.

findings.name: FortiGuard not connected
findings.description: FortiGuard Distribution Network is not connected. Security updates cannot be downloaded.
findings.recommendation: Verify FortiGuard connectivity. Check DNS, proxy, and firewall rules for FortiGuard servers.

findings.name: Automatic updates disabled
findings.description: Automatic signature updates are disabled. The FortiGate will not receive the latest threat definitions.
findings.recommendation: Enable automatic updates: config system autoupdate schedule / set status enable.

findings.name: Weak wireless security mode
findings.description: Wireless SSID 'Corp-Internal-WiFi' uses weak security mode 'wpa-personal'. Open/WEP/basic-WPA are trivially broken.
findings.recommendation: Configure WPA2-Enterprise (802.1X) or WPA3-SAE for all SSIDs.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config wireless-controller vap
  edit <ssid-name>
    set security wpa2-only-enterprise
  next
end

findings.name: Weak wireless security mode
findings.description: Wireless SSID 'Guest-WiFi' uses weak security mode 'open'. Open/WEP/basic-WPA are trivially broken.
findings.recommendation: Configure WPA2-Enterprise (802.1X) or WPA3-SAE for all SSIDs.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config wireless-controller vap
  edit <ssid-name>
    set security wpa2-only-enterprise
  next
end

findings.name: Guest SSID lacks client isolation
findings.description: Guest SSID 'Guest-WiFi' does not isolate wireless clients from each other.
findings.recommendation: Enable intra-vap-privacy (client isolation) on guest SSIDs to prevent lateral movement.

findings.name: Internal SSID broadcast enabled
findings.description: Internal/management SSID 'Corp-Internal-WiFi' broadcasts its SSID. This aids discovery by attackers.
findings.recommendation: Disable SSID broadcast for management and internal wireless networks.

findings.name: WIDS rogue AP scanning disabled
findings.description: WIDS profile 'default-wids' does not have rogue AP scanning enabled.
findings.recommendation: Enable ap-scan in the WIDS profile to detect unauthorized access points.

findings.name: No maximum client limit on SSID
findings.description: SSID 'Corp-Internal-WiFi' has no maximum client limit, allowing resource exhaustion via mass association.
findings.recommendation: Set a reasonable max-clients limit per SSID to prevent DoS via excessive client connections.

findings.name: No maximum client limit on SSID
findings.description: SSID 'Guest-WiFi' has no maximum client limit, allowing resource exhaustion via mass association.
findings.recommendation: Set a reasonable max-clients limit per SSID to prevent DoS via excessive client connections.

findings.name: Protected Management Frames disabled
findings.description: SSID 'Corp-Internal-WiFi' does not enforce 802.11w Protected Management Frames, leaving it vulnerable to deauthentication attacks.
findings.recommendation: Enable PMF (802.11w) — set to 'optional' for compatibility or 'required' for maximum protection.

findings.name: Protected Management Frames disabled
findings.description: SSID 'Guest-WiFi' does not enforce 802.11w Protected Management Frames, leaving it vulnerable to deauthentication attacks.
findings.recommendation: Enable PMF (802.11w) — set to 'optional' for compatibility or 'required' for maximum protection.

findings.name: CAPWAP tunnel using cleartext
findings.description: WTP profile 'default-wtp' allows cleartext CAPWAP tunnels. Wireless traffic between AP and controller is unencrypted.
findings.recommendation: Set dtls-policy to 'dtls-enabled' to encrypt CAPWAP data tunnels between APs and the controller.

findings.name: 802.11r FT-over-DS enabled
findings.description: SSID 'Corp-Internal-WiFi' has 802.11r Fast Transition over DS enabled, which has known vulnerabilities (CVE-2017-13082).
findings.recommendation: Disable ft-over-ds and use FT-over-Air instead for fast roaming.

findings.name: No FortiManager for centralised backup
findings.description: No FortiManager is configured for central configuration management and backup.
findings.recommendation: Register this FortiGate with FortiManager for automated config backups, change tracking, and disaster recovery.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system central-management
  set type fortimanager
  set fmg <fmg-ip>
end

findings.name: Config revision on logout disabled
findings.description: Automatic configuration revision backup on admin logout is disabled. Config changes may be lost.
findings.recommendation: Enable revision-backup-on-logout under system/global to maintain configuration history.

findings.name: HA session pickup disabled
findings.description: HA cluster does not have session pickup enabled. Active sessions will be dropped during failover.
findings.recommendation: Enable session-pickup in HA configuration to maintain sessions during failover events.

findings.name: Automatic config save without review
findings.description: Configuration changes are saved automatically without manual review, increasing risk of unintended changes persisting.
findings.recommendation: Consider setting cfg-save to 'revert' with a timeout to allow rollback of unintended changes.

findings.name: USB auto-install config enabled
findings.description: USB auto-install of configuration is enabled. A malicious USB device could overwrite the running configuration.
findings.recommendation: Disable auto-install-config and auto-install-image under system/global to prevent USB-based config tampering.

findings.name: LDAP server without TLS encryption
findings.description: LDAP server 'legacy-ldap' communicates in cleartext. Credentials and directory data are exposed on the network.
findings.recommendation: Enable LDAPS (port 636) or STARTTLS by setting secure to 'ldaps' or 'starttls'.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system admin
  edit <admin-name>
    set two-factor fortitoken
  next
end

findings.name: RADIUS shared secret too short
findings.description: RADIUS server 'radius-primary' shared secret is less than 16 characters, making it vulnerable to brute-force attacks.
findings.recommendation: Use a RADIUS shared secret of at least 22 characters with mixed character types.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config user ldap
  edit <ldap-server>
    set secure ldaps
    set server-identity-check enable
  next
end

findings.name: RADIUS shared secret too short
findings.description: RADIUS server 'radius-secondary' shared secret is less than 16 characters, making it vulnerable to brute-force attacks.
findings.recommendation: Use a RADIUS shared secret of at least 22 characters with mixed character types.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config user ldap
  edit <ldap-server>
    set secure ldaps
    set server-identity-check enable
  next
end

findings.name: RADIUS timeout excessively long
findings.description: RADIUS server 'radius-primary' has timeout of 60s. Excessive timeouts can cause authentication delays and DoS.
findings.recommendation: Set RADIUS timeout to 5-10 seconds and configure a secondary RADIUS server for redundancy.

findings.name: No SSO/SAML integration configured
findings.description: Neither SAML IdP nor Fortinet SSO (FSSO) is configured. Users rely solely on local or LDAP/RADIUS authentication.
findings.recommendation: Integrate SAML 2.0 SSO or FSSO for centralised identity management and conditional access.

findings.name: Local user without MFA
findings.description: Local user 'local-admin' does not have two-factor authentication enabled.
findings.recommendation: Enable two-factor authentication (FortiToken, email, or SMS) for all local user accounts.

findings.name: Local user without MFA
findings.description: Local user 'local-user1' does not have two-factor authentication enabled.
findings.recommendation: Enable two-factor authentication (FortiToken, email, or SMS) for all local user accounts.

findings.name: LDAP TLS without server identity check
findings.description: LDAP server 'corp-ad' uses TLS but does not verify the server certificate identity, allowing MITM attacks.
findings.recommendation: Enable server-identity-check on the LDAP server configuration to validate the TLS certificate.

findings.name: FIPS 140-2 mode not enabled
findings.description: FIPS 140-2 validated cryptography mode is not enabled. Environments requiring FIPS compliance (government, financial) must enable this.
findings.recommendation: Enable FIPS mode: config system global / set fips-cc enable. NOTE: Requires reboot and restricts cipher suites.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set fips-cc enable
end
# NOTE: Enabling FIPS mode requires reboot and restricts cipher suites.

findings.name: SCP admin access disabled
findings.description: SCP (Secure Copy Protocol) access for admin file transfer is disabled. SCP provides encrypted file transfer for config backup/restore.
findings.recommendation: Enable SCP access: config system global / set admin-scp enable.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set admin-scp enable
end

findings.name: TCP half-open session timer too long
findings.description: TCP half-open timer is 120s (recommended <=30). Long timers make the firewall vulnerable to SYN flood attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set tcp-halfopen-timer to 30 or less: config system global / set tcp-halfopen-timer 30.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set tcp-halfclose-timer 120
  set tcp-halfopen-timer 30
end

findings.name: TCP half-close session timer too long
findings.description: TCP half-close timer is 600s (recommended <=120). Excessive timers waste session table resources.
findings.recommendation: Set tcp-halfclose-timer to 120: config system global / set tcp-halfclose-timer 120.

findings.name: SSH grace time exceeds 120 seconds
findings.description: SSH grace time is 300s. This allows unauthenticated SSH sessions to remain open, consuming resources.
findings.recommendation: Set admin-ssh-grace-time to 60: config system global / set admin-ssh-grace-time 60.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config system global
  set admin-ssh-grace-time 60
end

findings.name: DNS queries not encrypted
findings.description: DNS queries are sent in cleartext, exposing internal hostname lookups to eavesdropping and manipulation.
findings.recommendation: Enable DNS over TLS (DoT) or DNS over HTTPS (DoH): config system dns / set protocol dot.

findings.name: MFA coverage: 0% of admin accounts
findings.description: Only 0 of 3 admin accounts (0%) have two-factor authentication enabled. All admin accounts should require MFA.
findings.recommendation: Enable FortiToken or email-based 2FA for all admin accounts: config system admin / edit <name> / set two-factor fortitoken.

findings.name: Default 'admin' account with super_admin profile
findings.description: The default 'admin' account exists with super_admin privileges. Best practice is to create named admin accounts and disable the default.
findings.recommendation: Create named admin accounts with appropriate profiles. Disable or rename the default 'admin' account.

findings.name: 4 active policies without security profiles
findings.description: 4 of 4 active policies (100%) have no AV, IPS, WebFilter, or other security profile attached. Traffic matching these rules bypasses all security inspection.
findings.recommendation: Attach security profiles (AV, IPS, Web Filter, Application Control) to all allow policies.

findings.name: 4 active policies with logging disabled
findings.description: 4 of 4 active policies have traffic logging disabled. This creates blind spots in security monitoring.
findings.recommendation: Enable logging on all policies: config firewall policy / edit <id> / set logtraffic all.

findings.name: Log transport not encrypted
findings.description: Logs are transported via unencrypted UDP. Log data may contain sensitive information visible to network eavesdroppers.
findings.recommendation: Use encrypted log transport (TCP with TLS) to FortiAnalyzer or syslog: config log fortianalyzer setting / set enc-algorithm high.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config log setting
  set log-file-size <size-MB>
end
Alternatively, configure FortiAnalyzer retention policies.

findings.name: Self-signed certificate in use: Fortinet_Factory_Self-Sign
findings.description: Certificate 'Fortinet_Factory_Self-Sign' is self-signed. Self-signed certificates are not trusted by clients and cannot be revoked.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a CA-signed certificate from a trusted Certificate Authority.

findings.name: Self-signed certificate in use: internal-ca
findings.description: Certificate 'internal-ca' is self-signed. Self-signed certificates are not trusted by clients and cannot be revoked.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a CA-signed certificate from a trusted Certificate Authority.

findings.name: Anti-spoofing disabled on wan interface: wan1
findings.description: Source IP address validation (anti-spoofing) is disabled on wan interface 'wan1'. This allows spoofed packets to traverse the firewall.
findings.recommendation: Enable anti-spoofing: config system interface / edit wan1 / set src-check enable.

findings.name: Anti-spoofing disabled on dmz interface: dmz
findings.description: Source IP address validation (anti-spoofing) is disabled on dmz interface 'dmz'. This allows spoofed packets to traverse the firewall.
findings.recommendation: Enable anti-spoofing: config system interface / edit dmz / set src-check enable.

findings.name: No automation stitches configured
findings.description: No automation stitches are configured. Automation stitches enable automatic response to security events (e.g., quarantine compromised hosts, alert on config changes).
findings.recommendation: Configure automation stitches for critical events: config system automation-trigger / edit <name>.
findings.remediation_cmd:
config log fortianalyzer setting
  set enc-algorithm high
end

findings.name: SD-WAN enabled without health checks
findings.description: SD-WAN is enabled but no health check probes are configured. Without health checks, failover between WAN links is blind to link quality.
findings.recommendation: Configure SD-WAN health checks with SLA targets: config system sdwan / config health-check.

findings.name: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application — IPS coverage gap
findings.description: MITRE T1190: Less than 50% of active firewall policies have IPS sensors attached. Exploits targeting public-facing applications (web servers, VPN portals, mail gateways) will not be detected on unprotected policies.
findings.recommendation: Attach IPS sensors to all policies allowing inbound traffic, especially those for DMZ/public-facing services.

findings.name: T1566 Phishing — AV/WebFilter coverage gap
findings.description: MITRE T1566: Antivirus and/or Web Filter profiles are not attached to the majority of policies. Phishing emails with malicious attachments or links may pass through without inspection.
findings.recommendation: Attach both AV and Web Filter profiles to all policies that allow user internet traffic.

findings.name: T1133 External Remote Services — SSL VPN without client cert
findings.description: MITRE T1133: SSL VPN is enabled without client certificate requirement. Password-only VPN authentication is vulnerable to credential stuffing, phishing, and brute force attacks.
findings.recommendation: Enable client certificate verification: config vpn ssl settings / set reqclientcert enable.

findings.name: T1059 Command & Scripting — Application Control coverage gap
findings.description: MITRE T1059: Application Control profiles are not widely deployed. Without Application Control, web shells, scripting engines, and malicious application traffic cannot be identified and blocked.
findings.recommendation: Create Application Control profiles that block high-risk categories and attach to all outbound/DMZ policies.

findings.name: T1203 Exploitation for Client Execution — no deep SSL inspection
findings.description: MITRE T1203: No SSL deep inspection profile detected. Without HTTPS inspection, exploit kits, drive-by downloads, and malicious payloads in encrypted traffic are invisible to IPS/AV scanning.
findings.recommendation: Create an SSL deep inspection profile and apply to policies for user internet traffic. Install the FortiGate CA cert on endpoints.

findings.name: T1078 Valid Accounts — admin accounts without MFA
findings.description: MITRE T1078: 3 admin account(s) lack two-factor authentication. Compromised credentials can grant full firewall control without additional verification.
findings.recommendation: Enable FortiToken or email-based 2FA for all admin accounts.

findings.name: T1071 Application Layer Protocol — DNS filter coverage gap
findings.description: MITRE T1071: DNS filtering is not widely deployed. Adversaries use DNS tunneling, DNS over HTTPS (DoH), and malicious domain resolution for C2 communication and data exfiltration.
findings.recommendation: Attach DNS Filter profiles (with botnet/C2 domain blocking) to all outbound policies.

findings.name: T1027 Obfuscated Files — no sandbox/outbreak prevention
findings.description: MITRE T1027: No AV profiles have sandbox analysis or outbreak prevention enabled. Obfuscated, packed, or zero-day malware will bypass signature-based detection.
findings.recommendation: Enable FortiGuard analytics (cloud sandbox) and outbreak prevention on AV profiles.

findings.name: T1562 Impair Defenses — no external log forwarding
findings.description: MITRE T1562: No external log forwarding (FortiAnalyzer or Syslog) is configured. An attacker who compromises the firewall can delete local logs, destroying evidence of the intrusion.
findings.recommendation: Configure log forwarding to FortiAnalyzer or external SIEM immediately. This is critical for incident response.

findings.name: T1110 Brute Force — no admin account lockout
findings.description: MITRE T1110: Admin account lockout is not configured. Adversaries can perform unlimited password guessing attempts against the management interface.
findings.recommendation: Set lockout: config system global / set admin-lockout-threshold 3 / set admin-lockout-duration 300.

findings.name: T1557 Adversary-in-the-Middle — HTTP on wan interface
findings.description: MITRE T1557: HTTP (unencrypted) is allowed on wan interface 'wan1'. This enables MitM attacks, credential interception, and session hijacking.
findings.recommendation: Remove HTTP from allowaccess on wan interfaces. Use HTTPS only with admin-https-redirect.

findings.name: T1021 Remote Services — 1 any/any/any policies allow lateral movement
findings.description: MITRE T1021: 1 firewall policies allow any source to any destination on any service. This permits unrestricted lateral movement (SMB, RDP, SSH, WMI) between network zones.
findings.recommendation: Replace any/any/any rules with specific source, destination, and service restrictions. Implement network segmentation.

findings.name: T1048 Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol — no DLP
findings.description: MITRE T1048: No DLP (Data Loss Prevention) sensors are attached to any firewall policies. Sensitive data can be exfiltrated via DNS, ICMP, HTTP, or custom protocols without detection.
findings.recommendation: Create DLP sensors to detect sensitive data patterns (SSN, credit cards, PII) and attach to outbound policies.

findings.name: T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 — DNS botnet detection disabled
findings.description: MITRE T1041: DNS botnet/C2 domain filtering is not enabled. Malware can use known C2 domains for command and control and data exfiltration without being blocked.
findings.recommendation: Enable botnet C2 domain blocking in DNS Filter profiles: config dnsfilter profile / set block-botnet enable.

findings.name: T1573 Encrypted Channel — encrypted C2 traffic invisible
findings.description: MITRE T1573: Without SSL deep inspection, adversaries can establish encrypted C2 channels over HTTPS that are completely invisible to IPS, AV, and application control.
findings.recommendation: Deploy SSL deep inspection for outbound user traffic. Whitelist trusted financial/medical sites via SSL exemption lists.

findings.name: T1090 Proxy — proxy/tunnel applications not blocked
findings.description: MITRE T1090: Application Control is not blocking proxy/tunnel applications (Tor, VPN tunnels, SOCKS proxies). Adversaries use these to disguise C2 traffic.
findings.recommendation: Block proxy/tunnel application categories in Application Control profiles.

findings.name: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact — no ransomware sandbox detection
findings.description: MITRE T1486: Cloud sandbox and outbreak prevention are disabled. Ransomware samples that evade signature detection (zero-day variants) will not be caught by behavioral analysis.
findings.recommendation: Enable FortiGuard sandbox analysis and outbreak prevention on all AV profiles protecting user traffic.

findings.name: T1595 Active Scanning — management exposed on WAN
findings.description: MITRE T1595: Management interfaces are exposed on WAN, allowing adversaries to discover, fingerprint, and attack the firewall's management plane directly from the internet. This is the #1 attack vector for FortiGate compromises (CVE-2024-55591, CVE-2024-21762).
findings.recommendation: Remove all management protocols from WAN interfaces immediately. Use a dedicated management VLAN or VPN-only admin access.

findings.name: T1189 Drive-by Compromise — Web Filter coverage insufficient
findings.description: MITRE T1189: Web Filter profiles cover less than 50% of policies. Users visiting compromised websites (watering holes) will not have malicious URLs blocked by FortiGuard URL categorization.
findings.recommendation: Attach Web Filter profiles to all policies allowing user internet traffic. Enable FortiGuard URL rating.

findings.name: T1046 Network Service Discovery — no inter-zone IPS
findings.line_content: No IPS sensor on inter-zone (east-west) policies
findings.description: MITRE T1046: No IPS sensors are applied to inter-zone policies. Internal network scanning (Nmap, masscan) by a compromised host traversing the firewall between zones will not trigger IPS detection signatures.
findings.recommendation: Apply IPS sensors to inter-zone (east-west) policies, not just inbound/outbound. This detects lateral movement scanning.

findings.name: T1210 Exploitation of Remote Services — east-west exploits undetected
findings.line_content: No IPS on inter-zone policies for exploit detection
findings.description: MITRE T1210: Without IPS on inter-zone policies, lateral exploitation (EternalBlue/MS17-010, PrintNightmare, Log4Shell targeting internal services) will not be detected as traffic passes between network zones.
findings.recommendation: Enable IPS with exploit signatures on all inter-zone policies to detect lateral exploitation attempts.

findings.name: T1219 Remote Access Software — RATs not blocked by AppCtrl
findings.description: MITRE T1219: Application Control is not blocking remote access tool categories (TeamViewer, AnyDesk, ScreenConnect, RustDesk). Adversaries deploy these legitimate tools as C2 channels to evade detection.
findings.recommendation: Block unauthorized remote access applications in AppCtrl profiles. Whitelist only sanctioned tools.

findings.name: T1568 Dynamic Resolution / DGA — DNS filter lacks category blocking
findings.line_content: dnsfilter 'dns-default' has no FTGD category filters
findings.description: MITRE T1568: DNS filter profile exists but has no FortiGuard DNS category filters configured. DGA-generated domains and newly registered malicious domains will not be caught by reputation scoring.
findings.recommendation: Configure FortiGuard DNS category filters to block malicious, phishing, and newly-registered domain categories.

findings.name: MITRE ATT&CK Resilience Score: 23%
findings.description: MITRE ATT&CK resilience score: 23% (7/30 controls properly configured, 23 gaps identified). Higher score = better defense against real-world attack techniques.
findings.recommendation: Address the 23 MITRE ATT&CK finding(s) above to improve resilience. Prioritize CRITICAL and HIGH findings first.
